findings.title: robots.txt Reveals 4 Sensitive Path(s)
findings.description: robots.txt lists paths that are disallowed from crawlers. These paths are revealed to attackers: /login, /signupemployer, /upload-cvs, /Dashboard
findings.remediation: Remove sensitive paths from robots.txt. Use authentication instead of obscurity.

findings.title: robots.txt Disallowed Path Accessible: /signupemployer
findings.description: The path /signupemployer listed in robots.txt as Disallow is publicly accessible (HTTP 200).
findings.remediation: Restrict this path with proper authentication, not robots.txt.

findings.title: robots.txt Disallowed Path Accessible: /login
findings.description: The path /login listed in robots.txt as Disallow is publicly accessible (HTTP 200).
findings.remediation: Restrict this path with proper authentication, not robots.txt.

findings.title: robots.txt Disallowed Path Accessible: /Dashboard
findings.description: The path /Dashboard listed in robots.txt as Disallow is publicly accessible (HTTP 200).
findings.remediation: Restrict this path with proper authentication, not robots.txt.

findings.title: robots.txt Disallowed Path Accessible: /upload-cvs
findings.description: The path /upload-cvs listed in robots.txt as Disallow is publicly accessible (HTTP 200).
findings.remediation: Restrict this path with proper authentication, not robots.txt.

findings.title: Exposed Endpoint: Actuator /heapdump
findings.description: /actuator/heapdump is publicly accessible. Actuator /heapdump endpoints can leak credentials, internal data, or application internals.
findings.remediation: Restrict /actuator/heapdump to authenticated users / internal network. Remove from public access.

findings.title: Exposed Endpoint: API v1 Root
findings.description: /api/v1 is publicly accessible. API v1 Root endpoints can leak credentials, internal data, or application internals.
findings.remediation: Restrict /api/v1 to authenticated users / internal network. Remove from public access.

findings.title: Exposed Endpoint: API v2 Root
findings.description: /api/v2 is publicly accessible. API v2 Root endpoints can leak credentials, internal data, or application internals.
findings.remediation: Restrict /api/v2 to authenticated users / internal network. Remove from public access.

findings.title: Exposed Endpoint: GraphQL Endpoint
findings.description: /graphql is publicly accessible. GraphQL Endpoint endpoints can leak credentials, internal data, or application internals.
findings.remediation: Restrict /graphql to authenticated users / internal network. Remove from public access.

findings.title: Exposed Endpoint: API v3 Root
findings.description: /api/v3 is publicly accessible. API v3 Root endpoints can leak credentials, internal data, or application internals.
findings.remediation: Restrict /api/v3 to authenticated users / internal network. Remove from public access.

findings.title: Exposed Endpoint: Debug Endpoint
findings.description: /debug is publicly accessible. Debug Endpoint endpoints can leak credentials, internal data, or application internals.
findings.remediation: Restrict /debug to authenticated users / internal network. Remove from public access.

findings.title: Exposed Endpoint: Info Endpoint
findings.description: /info is publicly accessible. Info Endpoint endpoints can leak credentials, internal data, or application internals.
findings.remediation: Restrict /info to authenticated users / internal network. Remove from public access.
findings.owasp: A09:2021 Security Logging and Monitoring Failures

findings.title: Exposed Endpoint: Env Endpoint
findings.description: /env is publicly accessible. Env Endpoint endpoints can leak credentials, internal data, or application internals.
findings.remediation: Restrict /env to authenticated users / internal network. Remove from public access.

findings.title: Server Version Disclosed: AmazonS3
findings.description: The Server header reveals the web server software version, aiding targeted attacks.
findings.remediation: Remove or genericise the Server header in web server config.

checks.name: Version Check
checks.value: No known vulnerable versions detected in headers

checks.name: Form Protection
checks.value: No POST forms found on homepage

findings.title: Missing Header: Strict-Transport-Security
findings.description: HSTS not set — browsers can downgrade to HTTP
findings.remediation: Add: Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=63072000; includeSubDomains; preload

findings.title: Missing Header: Content-Security-Policy
findings.description: No CSP — XSS/data injection unmitigated
findings.remediation: Add: Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'

findings.title: Missing Header: X-Frame-Options
findings.description: Clickjacking protection missing
findings.remediation: Add: X-Frame-Options: DENY

findings.title: Missing Header: X-Content-Type-Options
findings.description: MIME-sniffing not prevented
findings.remediation: Add: X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff

findings.title: Missing Header: Referrer-Policy
findings.description: Referrer policy not set
findings.remediation: Add: Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin

findings.title: Missing Header: Permissions-Policy
findings.description: Permissions policy not configured
findings.remediation: Add Permissions-Policy header to restrict browser features

findings.title: Missing Header: X-XSS-Protection
findings.description: Legacy XSS header missing (prefer CSP)
findings.remediation: Add X-XSS-Protection: 0 and rely on CSP

findings.title: robots.txt Reveals 4 Sensitive Path(s)
findings.description: robots.txt lists paths that are disallowed from crawlers. These paths are revealed to attackers: /login, /signupemployer, /upload-cvs, /Dashboard
findings.remediation: Remove sensitive paths from robots.txt. Use authentication instead of obscurity.

findings.title: Server Version Disclosed: AmazonS3
findings.description: The Server header reveals the web server software version, aiding targeted attacks.
findings.remediation: Remove or genericise the Server header in web server config.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: Kubernetes Dashboard
findings.description: Kubernetes Dashboard is publicly accessible. May allow full cluster access without authentication. Related CVE: CVE-2018-18264.
findings.remediation: Disable public access. Enforce RBAC. Use kubectl proxy for access.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: Portainer (Docker UI)
findings.description: Portainer Docker management UI is publicly accessible. Provides full Docker host control including container creation.
findings.remediation: Restrict Portainer to internal network. Enable TLS and authentication.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: RabbitMQ Management
findings.description: RabbitMQ Management UI is publicly accessible. Default guest/guest credentials may allow queue control.
findings.remediation: Change default credentials. Bind management plugin to localhost.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: Apache Tomcat Manager
findings.description: Tomcat Manager is publicly accessible. Default credentials (tomcat/tomcat) may allow deploying a WAR shell (RCE). Related CVE: CVE-2020-1938.
findings.remediation: Restrict /manager to localhost. Change default credentials. Enforce IP allowlist.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: phpMyAdmin
findings.description: phpMyAdmin database management interface is publicly accessible. Allows full database access. Related CVE: CVE-2019-12616.
findings.remediation: Restrict phpMyAdmin to internal IPs. Use strong authentication.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: MinIO Console (S3)
findings.description: MinIO S3-compatible object storage console is publicly accessible.
findings.remediation: Restrict MinIO to internal network. Change default minioadmin/minioadmin credentials.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: Jenkins CI Server
findings.description: Jenkins dashboard is publicly accessible. Unauthenticated Jenkins installations allow script console RCE. Related CVE: CVE-2024-23897.
findings.remediation: Enable Jenkins security. Restrict access. Upgrade to latest version.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: Prometheus Metrics
findings.description: Prometheus metrics endpoint is publicly accessible. Leaks internal service names, versions, and infrastructure details.
findings.remediation: Restrict /metrics endpoint to internal monitoring systems. Add authentication.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: Elasticsearch API
findings.description: Elasticsearch REST API is publicly accessible without authentication. Full data access and cluster control. Related CVE: CVE-2015-1427.
findings.remediation: Enable X-Pack security. Bind Elasticsearch to localhost or internal network only.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: GitLab Instance
findings.description: GitLab login page is publicly accessible. Misconfigured GitLab instances expose source code. Related CVE: CVE-2021-22205.
findings.remediation: Restrict GitLab to internal network or VPN. Enable 2FA. Keep updated.

findings.title: 🎯 CONFIRMED: CVE-2017-5638 — Apache Struts 2 RCE (Content-Type)
findings.description: Apache Struts 2 (CVE-2017-5638): OGNL injection via Content-Type header returned command output — RCE confirmed.
findings.remediation: Upgrade Apache Struts to 2.3.35+ or 2.5.17+. Implement WAF rules for OGNL injection.

findings.title: No Rate Limiting on: /api/login
findings.description: The /api/login endpoint does not return rate-limiting headers. Without rate limiting, brute-force and credential stuffing attacks are unrestricted.
findings.remediation: Implement rate limiting (e.g., 5 req/min per IP). Return X-RateLimit-Limit headers. Use CAPTCHA on repeated failures.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: Apache Tomcat Manager
findings.description: Tomcat Manager is publicly accessible. Default credentials (tomcat/tomcat) may allow deploying a WAR shell (RCE). Related CVE: CVE-2020-1938.
findings.remediation: Restrict /manager to localhost. Change default credentials. Enforce IP allowlist.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: Webmin Admin
findings.description: Webmin web-based system administration interface is publicly accessible. Related CVE: CVE-2019-15107.
findings.remediation: Restrict Webmin access to trusted IPs. Enable two-factor authentication.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: Vault UI (HashiCorp)
findings.description: HashiCorp Vault is publicly accessible. Leaks secret storage engine information.
findings.remediation: Restrict Vault to internal network. Enforce strict policies.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: Jenkins CI Server
findings.description: Jenkins dashboard is publicly accessible. Unauthenticated Jenkins installations allow script console RCE. Related CVE: CVE-2024-23897.
findings.remediation: Enable Jenkins security. Restrict access. Upgrade to latest version.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: Prometheus Metrics
findings.description: Prometheus metrics endpoint is publicly accessible. Leaks internal service names, versions, and infrastructure details.
findings.remediation: Restrict /metrics endpoint to internal monitoring systems. Add authentication.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: RabbitMQ Management
findings.description: RabbitMQ Management UI is publicly accessible. Default guest/guest credentials may allow queue control.
findings.remediation: Change default credentials. Bind management plugin to localhost.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: Grafana Dashboard
findings.description: Grafana dashboard is publicly accessible. Default admin/admin credentials allow full takeover. Related CVE: CVE-2021-43798.
findings.remediation: Change default Grafana credentials. Restrict access to internal network.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: Kibana Dashboard
findings.description: Kibana is publicly accessible. Unauthenticated Kibana exposes all indexed data and supports script execution. Related CVE: CVE-2019-7616.
findings.remediation: Enable X-Pack security. Restrict Kibana to internal network.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: Elasticsearch API
findings.description: Elasticsearch REST API is publicly accessible without authentication. Full data access and cluster control. Related CVE: CVE-2015-1427.
findings.remediation: Enable X-Pack security. Bind Elasticsearch to localhost or internal network only.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: Redis Commander
findings.description: Redis Commander web UI is publicly accessible. Allows browsing and modifying all Redis keys.
findings.remediation: Add authentication to Redis Commander. Restrict to internal network.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: Portainer (Docker UI)
findings.description: Portainer Docker management UI is publicly accessible. Provides full Docker host control including container creation.
findings.remediation: Restrict Portainer to internal network. Enable TLS and authentication.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: Nexus Repository Manager
findings.description: Sonatype Nexus repository manager is publicly accessible. Allows artifact browsing and potential supply chain attacks. Related CVE: CVE-2019-7238.
findings.remediation: Restrict Nexus to internal network. Change default admin/admin123 credentials.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: Laravel Horizon
findings.description: Laravel Horizon queue dashboard is publicly accessible. Exposes all job data and allows job manipulation.
findings.remediation: Add authentication gate to Horizon. Restrict to admin users.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: SonarQube
findings.description: SonarQube code quality dashboard is publicly accessible. Exposes source code analysis and potential vulnerability data. Related CVE: CVE-2021-27917.
findings.remediation: Restrict SonarQube to internal network. Enable authentication.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: Traefik Dashboard
findings.description: Traefik reverse proxy dashboard is publicly accessible. Leaks routing rules, backend services, and middleware config.
findings.remediation: Protect Traefik dashboard with basic auth or OAuth. Disable on public-facing interfaces.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: Apache Solr
findings.description: Apache Solr admin interface is publicly accessible. Allows arbitrary file reads and remote code execution via VelocityResponseWriter. Related CVE: CVE-2019-0193.
findings.remediation: Restrict Solr to internal network. Enable authentication (Solr 8.x).

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: Consul UI
findings.description: HashiCorp Consul UI/API is publicly accessible. Exposes service registry and health data.
findings.remediation: Enable Consul ACL system. Bind to internal interface only.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: Kubernetes Dashboard
findings.description: Kubernetes Dashboard is publicly accessible. May allow full cluster access without authentication. Related CVE: CVE-2018-18264.
findings.remediation: Disable public access. Enforce RBAC. Use kubectl proxy for access.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: GitLab Instance
findings.description: GitLab login page is publicly accessible. Misconfigured GitLab instances expose source code. Related CVE: CVE-2021-22205.
findings.remediation: Restrict GitLab to internal network or VPN. Enable 2FA. Keep updated.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: Adminer (DB Tool)
findings.description: Adminer database tool is publicly accessible. Supports connecting to any database server. Related CVE: CVE-2021-43008.
findings.remediation: Remove Adminer from production. Restrict access by IP.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: phpMyAdmin
findings.description: phpMyAdmin database management interface is publicly accessible. Allows full database access. Related CVE: CVE-2019-12616.
findings.remediation: Restrict phpMyAdmin to internal IPs. Use strong authentication.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: MinIO Console (S3)
findings.description: MinIO S3-compatible object storage console is publicly accessible.
findings.remediation: Restrict MinIO to internal network. Change default minioadmin/minioadmin credentials.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: ArgoCD Dashboard
findings.description: ArgoCD GitOps deployment dashboard is publicly accessible. Provides Kubernetes deployment control. Related CVE: CVE-2022-29165.
findings.remediation: Enable ArgoCD RBAC. Restrict to internal network. Enable SSO.

findings.title: No Rate Limiting on: /api/login
findings.description: The /api/login endpoint does not return rate-limiting headers. Without rate limiting, brute-force and credential stuffing attacks are unrestricted.
findings.remediation: Implement rate limiting (e.g., 5 req/min per IP). Return X-RateLimit-Limit headers. Use CAPTCHA on repeated failures.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: Portainer (Docker UI)
findings.description: Portainer Docker management UI is publicly accessible. Provides full Docker host control including container creation.
findings.remediation: Restrict Portainer to internal network. Enable TLS and authentication.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: Nexus Repository Manager
findings.description: Sonatype Nexus repository manager is publicly accessible. Allows artifact browsing and potential supply chain attacks. Related CVE: CVE-2019-7238.
findings.remediation: Restrict Nexus to internal network. Change default admin/admin123 credentials.

findings.title: No Rate Limiting on: /api/login
findings.description: The /api/login endpoint does not return rate-limiting headers. Without rate limiting, brute-force and credential stuffing attacks are unrestricted.
findings.remediation: Implement rate limiting (e.g., 5 req/min per IP). Return X-RateLimit-Limit headers. Use CAPTCHA on repeated failures.

secrets.name: Generic Secret / Password in Code
secrets.desc: Hardcoded credential or secret value found in JavaScript.
secrets.fix: Remove hardcoded credentials. Use environment variables and server-side secret management.

findings.title: Secret Exposed in JavaScript: Generic Secret / Password in Code
findings.description:
Hardcoded credential or secret value found in JavaScript. Found in: https://staging.worknigeria.com/scripts.js
Line ~4616. Masked value: .data-ap•
findings.remediation: Remove hardcoded credentials. Use environment variables and server-side secret management.

findings.title: security.txt Not Found
findings.description: RFC 9116 recommends publishing a security.txt file to provide vulnerability disclosure contacts.
findings.remediation: Create /.well-known/security.txt with Contact: and Expires: fields per RFC 9116.

findings.title: Missing Subresource Integrity (SRI) on External Scripts
findings.description: 3 external script(s) lack SRI integrity attributes, making them vulnerable to CDN supply chain attacks.
findings.remediation: Add integrity and crossorigin attributes to all external scripts. Use https://www.srihash.org/ to generate hashes.

findings.title: DMARC Policy Set to None
findings.description: DMARC is configured but with p=none, meaning no action is taken on failing emails.
findings.remediation: Graduate DMARC policy from p=none to p=quarantine, then p=reject after reviewing reports.

findings.title: No DKIM Records Found
findings.description: No DKIM signing records detected for common selectors.
findings.remediation: Configure DKIM signing in your email provider and publish the public key as a TXT record.

findings.title: DNSSEC Not Enabled
findings.description: DNSSEC is not configured, leaving DNS responses vulnerable to cache poisoning attacks.
findings.remediation: Enable DNSSEC through your domain registrar and DNS provider.

findings.title: Missing SPF Record
findings.description: No SPF record found for www.worknigeria.com. This allows anyone to send email on behalf of this domain.
findings.remediation: Add a TXT record: v=spf1 include:_spf.yourmailprovider.com ~all

findings.title: Missing DMARC Record
findings.description: No DMARC record found. Email spoofing cannot be detected or blocked.
findings.remediation: Add: _dmarc.yourdomain.com TXT "v=DMARC1; p=quarantine; rua=mailto:dmarc@yourdomain.com"

findings.title: Missing Header: Strict-Transport-Security
findings.description: HSTS not set — browsers can downgrade to HTTP
findings.remediation: Add: Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=63072000; includeSubDomains; preload

findings.title: Missing Header: Content-Security-Policy
findings.description: No CSP — XSS/data injection unmitigated
findings.remediation: Add: Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'

findings.title: Missing Header: X-Frame-Options
findings.description: Clickjacking protection missing
findings.remediation: Add: X-Frame-Options: DENY

findings.title: Missing Header: X-Content-Type-Options
findings.description: MIME-sniffing not prevented
findings.remediation: Add: X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff

findings.title: Missing Header: Referrer-Policy
findings.description: Referrer policy not set
findings.remediation: Add: Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin

findings.title: Missing Header: Permissions-Policy
findings.description: Permissions policy not configured
findings.remediation: Add Permissions-Policy header to restrict browser features

findings.title: Missing Header: X-XSS-Protection
findings.description: Legacy XSS header missing (prefer CSP)
findings.remediation: Add X-XSS-Protection: 0 and rely on CSP

findings.title: robots.txt Reveals 4 Sensitive Path(s)
findings.description: robots.txt lists paths that are disallowed from crawlers. These paths are revealed to attackers: /login, /signupemployer, /upload-cvs, /Dashboard
findings.remediation: Remove sensitive paths from robots.txt. Use authentication instead of obscurity.

findings.title: robots.txt Disallowed Path Accessible: /login
findings.description: The path /login listed in robots.txt as Disallow is publicly accessible (HTTP 200).
findings.remediation: Restrict this path with proper authentication, not robots.txt.

findings.title: robots.txt Disallowed Path Accessible: /signupemployer
findings.description: The path /signupemployer listed in robots.txt as Disallow is publicly accessible (HTTP 200).
findings.remediation: Restrict this path with proper authentication, not robots.txt.

findings.title: Exposed Endpoint: Actuator /heapdump
findings.description: /actuator/heapdump is publicly accessible. Actuator /heapdump endpoints can leak credentials, internal data, or application internals.
findings.remediation: Restrict /actuator/heapdump to authenticated users / internal network. Remove from public access.

findings.title: Exposed Endpoint: Info Endpoint
findings.description: /info is publicly accessible. Info Endpoint endpoints can leak credentials, internal data, or application internals.
findings.remediation: Restrict /info to authenticated users / internal network. Remove from public access.
findings.owasp: A09:2021 Security Logging and Monitoring Failures

findings.title: Exposed Endpoint: Env Endpoint
findings.description: /env is publicly accessible. Env Endpoint endpoints can leak credentials, internal data, or application internals.
findings.remediation: Restrict /env to authenticated users / internal network. Remove from public access.

findings.title: Exposed Endpoint: GraphQL Endpoint
findings.description: /graphql is publicly accessible. GraphQL Endpoint endpoints can leak credentials, internal data, or application internals.
findings.remediation: Restrict /graphql to authenticated users / internal network. Remove from public access.

findings.title: Exposed Endpoint: API v1 Root
findings.description: /api/v1 is publicly accessible. API v1 Root endpoints can leak credentials, internal data, or application internals.
findings.remediation: Restrict /api/v1 to authenticated users / internal network. Remove from public access.

findings.title: Exposed Endpoint: API v2 Root
findings.description: /api/v2 is publicly accessible. API v2 Root endpoints can leak credentials, internal data, or application internals.
findings.remediation: Restrict /api/v2 to authenticated users / internal network. Remove from public access.

findings.title: Exposed Endpoint: API v3 Root
findings.description: /api/v3 is publicly accessible. API v3 Root endpoints can leak credentials, internal data, or application internals.
findings.remediation: Restrict /api/v3 to authenticated users / internal network. Remove from public access.

findings.title: Server Version Disclosed: AmazonS3
findings.description: The Server header reveals the web server software version, aiding targeted attacks.
findings.remediation: Remove or genericise the Server header in web server config.

findings.title: Missing Header: Strict-Transport-Security
findings.description: HSTS not set — browsers can downgrade to HTTP
findings.remediation: Add: Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=63072000; includeSubDomains; preload

findings.title: Missing Header: Content-Security-Policy
findings.description: No CSP — XSS/data injection unmitigated
findings.remediation: Add: Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'

findings.title: Missing Header: X-Frame-Options
findings.description: Clickjacking protection missing
findings.remediation: Add: X-Frame-Options: DENY

findings.title: Missing Header: X-Content-Type-Options
findings.description: MIME-sniffing not prevented
findings.remediation: Add: X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff

findings.title: Missing Header: Referrer-Policy
findings.description: Referrer policy not set
findings.remediation: Add: Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin

findings.title: Missing Header: Permissions-Policy
findings.description: Permissions policy not configured
findings.remediation: Add Permissions-Policy header to restrict browser features

findings.title: Missing Header: X-XSS-Protection
findings.description: Legacy XSS header missing (prefer CSP)
findings.remediation: Add X-XSS-Protection: 0 and rely on CSP

findings.title: Dangerous HTTP Method Allowed: PUT
findings.description: PUT returned 200. This may expose CSRF/XST attack surface.
findings.remediation: Disable the PUT method on the web server if not required.

findings.title: Dangerous HTTP Method Allowed: DELETE
findings.description: DELETE returned 200. This may expose CSRF/XST attack surface.
findings.remediation: Disable the DELETE method on the web server if not required.

findings.title: Dangerous HTTP Method Allowed: PATCH
findings.description: PATCH returned 200. This may expose CSRF/XST attack surface.
findings.remediation: Disable the PATCH method on the web server if not required.

findings.title: robots.txt Reveals 4 Sensitive Path(s)
findings.description: robots.txt lists paths that are disallowed from crawlers. These paths are revealed to attackers: /login, /signupemployer, /upload-cvs, /Dashboard
findings.remediation: Remove sensitive paths from robots.txt. Use authentication instead of obscurity.

findings.title: robots.txt Disallowed Path Accessible: /signupemployer
findings.description: The path /signupemployer listed in robots.txt as Disallow is publicly accessible (HTTP 200).
findings.remediation: Restrict this path with proper authentication, not robots.txt.

findings.title: robots.txt Disallowed Path Accessible: /login
findings.description: The path /login listed in robots.txt as Disallow is publicly accessible (HTTP 200).
findings.remediation: Restrict this path with proper authentication, not robots.txt.

findings.title: robots.txt Disallowed Path Accessible: /Dashboard
findings.description: The path /Dashboard listed in robots.txt as Disallow is publicly accessible (HTTP 200).
findings.remediation: Restrict this path with proper authentication, not robots.txt.

findings.title: robots.txt Disallowed Path Accessible: /upload-cvs
findings.description: The path /upload-cvs listed in robots.txt as Disallow is publicly accessible (HTTP 200).
findings.remediation: Restrict this path with proper authentication, not robots.txt.

findings.title: Exposed Endpoint: Actuator /heapdump
findings.description: /actuator/heapdump is publicly accessible. Actuator /heapdump endpoints can leak credentials, internal data, or application internals.
findings.remediation: Restrict /actuator/heapdump to authenticated users / internal network. Remove from public access.

findings.title: Exposed Endpoint: API v1 Root
findings.description: /api/v1 is publicly accessible. API v1 Root endpoints can leak credentials, internal data, or application internals.
findings.remediation: Restrict /api/v1 to authenticated users / internal network. Remove from public access.

findings.title: Exposed Endpoint: API v2 Root
findings.description: /api/v2 is publicly accessible. API v2 Root endpoints can leak credentials, internal data, or application internals.
findings.remediation: Restrict /api/v2 to authenticated users / internal network. Remove from public access.

findings.title: Exposed Endpoint: GraphQL Endpoint
findings.description: /graphql is publicly accessible. GraphQL Endpoint endpoints can leak credentials, internal data, or application internals.
findings.remediation: Restrict /graphql to authenticated users / internal network. Remove from public access.

findings.title: Exposed Endpoint: API v3 Root
findings.description: /api/v3 is publicly accessible. API v3 Root endpoints can leak credentials, internal data, or application internals.
findings.remediation: Restrict /api/v3 to authenticated users / internal network. Remove from public access.

findings.title: Exposed Endpoint: Debug Endpoint
findings.description: /debug is publicly accessible. Debug Endpoint endpoints can leak credentials, internal data, or application internals.
findings.remediation: Restrict /debug to authenticated users / internal network. Remove from public access.

findings.title: Exposed Endpoint: Info Endpoint
findings.description: /info is publicly accessible. Info Endpoint endpoints can leak credentials, internal data, or application internals.
findings.remediation: Restrict /info to authenticated users / internal network. Remove from public access.
findings.owasp: A09:2021 Security Logging and Monitoring Failures

findings.title: Exposed Endpoint: Env Endpoint
findings.description: /env is publicly accessible. Env Endpoint endpoints can leak credentials, internal data, or application internals.
findings.remediation: Restrict /env to authenticated users / internal network. Remove from public access.

findings.title: Server Version Disclosed: AmazonS3
findings.description: The Server header reveals the web server software version, aiding targeted attacks.
findings.remediation: Remove or genericise the Server header in web server config.

findings.title: Missing Header: Strict-Transport-Security
findings.description: HSTS not set — browsers can downgrade to HTTP
findings.remediation: Add: Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=63072000; includeSubDomains; preload

findings.title: Missing Header: Content-Security-Policy
findings.description: No CSP — XSS/data injection unmitigated
findings.remediation: Add: Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'

findings.title: Missing Header: X-Frame-Options
findings.description: Clickjacking protection missing
findings.remediation: Add: X-Frame-Options: DENY

findings.title: Missing Header: X-Content-Type-Options
findings.description: MIME-sniffing not prevented
findings.remediation: Add: X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff

findings.title: Missing Header: Referrer-Policy
findings.description: Referrer policy not set
findings.remediation: Add: Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin

findings.title: Missing Header: Permissions-Policy
findings.description: Permissions policy not configured
findings.remediation: Add Permissions-Policy header to restrict browser features

findings.title: Missing Header: X-XSS-Protection
findings.description: Legacy XSS header missing (prefer CSP)
findings.remediation: Add X-XSS-Protection: 0 and rely on CSP

findings.title: robots.txt Reveals 4 Sensitive Path(s)
findings.description: robots.txt lists paths that are disallowed from crawlers. These paths are revealed to attackers: /login, /signupemployer, /upload-cvs, /Dashboard
findings.remediation: Remove sensitive paths from robots.txt. Use authentication instead of obscurity.

findings.title: Server Version Disclosed: AmazonS3
findings.description: The Server header reveals the web server software version, aiding targeted attacks.
findings.remediation: Remove or genericise the Server header in web server config.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: Kubernetes Dashboard
findings.description: Kubernetes Dashboard is publicly accessible. May allow full cluster access without authentication. Related CVE: CVE-2018-18264.
findings.remediation: Disable public access. Enforce RBAC. Use kubectl proxy for access.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: Portainer (Docker UI)
findings.description: Portainer Docker management UI is publicly accessible. Provides full Docker host control including container creation.
findings.remediation: Restrict Portainer to internal network. Enable TLS and authentication.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: RabbitMQ Management
findings.description: RabbitMQ Management UI is publicly accessible. Default guest/guest credentials may allow queue control.
findings.remediation: Change default credentials. Bind management plugin to localhost.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: Apache Tomcat Manager
findings.description: Tomcat Manager is publicly accessible. Default credentials (tomcat/tomcat) may allow deploying a WAR shell (RCE). Related CVE: CVE-2020-1938.
findings.remediation: Restrict /manager to localhost. Change default credentials. Enforce IP allowlist.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: phpMyAdmin
findings.description: phpMyAdmin database management interface is publicly accessible. Allows full database access. Related CVE: CVE-2019-12616.
findings.remediation: Restrict phpMyAdmin to internal IPs. Use strong authentication.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: MinIO Console (S3)
findings.description: MinIO S3-compatible object storage console is publicly accessible.
findings.remediation: Restrict MinIO to internal network. Change default minioadmin/minioadmin credentials.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: Jenkins CI Server
findings.description: Jenkins dashboard is publicly accessible. Unauthenticated Jenkins installations allow script console RCE. Related CVE: CVE-2024-23897.
findings.remediation: Enable Jenkins security. Restrict access. Upgrade to latest version.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: Prometheus Metrics
findings.description: Prometheus metrics endpoint is publicly accessible. Leaks internal service names, versions, and infrastructure details.
findings.remediation: Restrict /metrics endpoint to internal monitoring systems. Add authentication.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: Elasticsearch API
findings.description: Elasticsearch REST API is publicly accessible without authentication. Full data access and cluster control. Related CVE: CVE-2015-1427.
findings.remediation: Enable X-Pack security. Bind Elasticsearch to localhost or internal network only.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: GitLab Instance
findings.description: GitLab login page is publicly accessible. Misconfigured GitLab instances expose source code. Related CVE: CVE-2021-22205.
findings.remediation: Restrict GitLab to internal network or VPN. Enable 2FA. Keep updated.

findings.title: 🎯 CONFIRMED: CVE-2017-5638 — Apache Struts 2 RCE (Content-Type)
findings.description: Apache Struts 2 (CVE-2017-5638): OGNL injection via Content-Type header returned command output — RCE confirmed.
findings.remediation: Upgrade Apache Struts to 2.3.35+ or 2.5.17+. Implement WAF rules for OGNL injection.

findings.title: No Rate Limiting on: /api/login
findings.description: The /api/login endpoint does not return rate-limiting headers. Without rate limiting, brute-force and credential stuffing attacks are unrestricted.
findings.remediation: Implement rate limiting (e.g., 5 req/min per IP). Return X-RateLimit-Limit headers. Use CAPTCHA on repeated failures.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: Apache Tomcat Manager
findings.description: Tomcat Manager is publicly accessible. Default credentials (tomcat/tomcat) may allow deploying a WAR shell (RCE). Related CVE: CVE-2020-1938.
findings.remediation: Restrict /manager to localhost. Change default credentials. Enforce IP allowlist.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: Webmin Admin
findings.description: Webmin web-based system administration interface is publicly accessible. Related CVE: CVE-2019-15107.
findings.remediation: Restrict Webmin access to trusted IPs. Enable two-factor authentication.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: Vault UI (HashiCorp)
findings.description: HashiCorp Vault is publicly accessible. Leaks secret storage engine information.
findings.remediation: Restrict Vault to internal network. Enforce strict policies.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: Jenkins CI Server
findings.description: Jenkins dashboard is publicly accessible. Unauthenticated Jenkins installations allow script console RCE. Related CVE: CVE-2024-23897.
findings.remediation: Enable Jenkins security. Restrict access. Upgrade to latest version.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: Prometheus Metrics
findings.description: Prometheus metrics endpoint is publicly accessible. Leaks internal service names, versions, and infrastructure details.
findings.remediation: Restrict /metrics endpoint to internal monitoring systems. Add authentication.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: RabbitMQ Management
findings.description: RabbitMQ Management UI is publicly accessible. Default guest/guest credentials may allow queue control.
findings.remediation: Change default credentials. Bind management plugin to localhost.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: Grafana Dashboard
findings.description: Grafana dashboard is publicly accessible. Default admin/admin credentials allow full takeover. Related CVE: CVE-2021-43798.
findings.remediation: Change default Grafana credentials. Restrict access to internal network.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: Kibana Dashboard
findings.description: Kibana is publicly accessible. Unauthenticated Kibana exposes all indexed data and supports script execution. Related CVE: CVE-2019-7616.
findings.remediation: Enable X-Pack security. Restrict Kibana to internal network.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: Elasticsearch API
findings.description: Elasticsearch REST API is publicly accessible without authentication. Full data access and cluster control. Related CVE: CVE-2015-1427.
findings.remediation: Enable X-Pack security. Bind Elasticsearch to localhost or internal network only.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: Redis Commander
findings.description: Redis Commander web UI is publicly accessible. Allows browsing and modifying all Redis keys.
findings.remediation: Add authentication to Redis Commander. Restrict to internal network.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: Portainer (Docker UI)
findings.description: Portainer Docker management UI is publicly accessible. Provides full Docker host control including container creation.
findings.remediation: Restrict Portainer to internal network. Enable TLS and authentication.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: Nexus Repository Manager
findings.description: Sonatype Nexus repository manager is publicly accessible. Allows artifact browsing and potential supply chain attacks. Related CVE: CVE-2019-7238.
findings.remediation: Restrict Nexus to internal network. Change default admin/admin123 credentials.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: Laravel Horizon
findings.description: Laravel Horizon queue dashboard is publicly accessible. Exposes all job data and allows job manipulation.
findings.remediation: Add authentication gate to Horizon. Restrict to admin users.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: SonarQube
findings.description: SonarQube code quality dashboard is publicly accessible. Exposes source code analysis and potential vulnerability data. Related CVE: CVE-2021-27917.
findings.remediation: Restrict SonarQube to internal network. Enable authentication.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: Traefik Dashboard
findings.description: Traefik reverse proxy dashboard is publicly accessible. Leaks routing rules, backend services, and middleware config.
findings.remediation: Protect Traefik dashboard with basic auth or OAuth. Disable on public-facing interfaces.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: Apache Solr
findings.description: Apache Solr admin interface is publicly accessible. Allows arbitrary file reads and remote code execution via VelocityResponseWriter. Related CVE: CVE-2019-0193.
findings.remediation: Restrict Solr to internal network. Enable authentication (Solr 8.x).

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: Consul UI
findings.description: HashiCorp Consul UI/API is publicly accessible. Exposes service registry and health data.
findings.remediation: Enable Consul ACL system. Bind to internal interface only.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: Kubernetes Dashboard
findings.description: Kubernetes Dashboard is publicly accessible. May allow full cluster access without authentication. Related CVE: CVE-2018-18264.
findings.remediation: Disable public access. Enforce RBAC. Use kubectl proxy for access.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: GitLab Instance
findings.description: GitLab login page is publicly accessible. Misconfigured GitLab instances expose source code. Related CVE: CVE-2021-22205.
findings.remediation: Restrict GitLab to internal network or VPN. Enable 2FA. Keep updated.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: Adminer (DB Tool)
findings.description: Adminer database tool is publicly accessible. Supports connecting to any database server. Related CVE: CVE-2021-43008.
findings.remediation: Remove Adminer from production. Restrict access by IP.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: phpMyAdmin
findings.description: phpMyAdmin database management interface is publicly accessible. Allows full database access. Related CVE: CVE-2019-12616.
findings.remediation: Restrict phpMyAdmin to internal IPs. Use strong authentication.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: MinIO Console (S3)
findings.description: MinIO S3-compatible object storage console is publicly accessible.
findings.remediation: Restrict MinIO to internal network. Change default minioadmin/minioadmin credentials.

findings.title: Exposed Management Panel: ArgoCD Dashboard
findings.description: ArgoCD GitOps deployment dashboard is publicly accessible. Provides Kubernetes deployment control. Related CVE: CVE-2022-29165.
findings.remediation: Enable ArgoCD RBAC. Restrict to internal network. Enable SSO.

findings.title: Secret Exposed in JavaScript: Generic Secret / Password in Code
findings.description:
Hardcoded credential or secret value found in JavaScript. Found in: https://staging.worknigeria.com/scripts.js
Line ~4616. Masked value: .data-ap•
findings.remediation: Remove hardcoded credentials. Use environment variables and server-side secret management.

findings.title: security.txt Not Found
findings.description: RFC 9116 recommends publishing a security.txt file to provide vulnerability disclosure contacts.
findings.remediation: Create /.well-known/security.txt with Contact: and Expires: fields per RFC 9116.

findings.title: Missing Subresource Integrity (SRI) on External Scripts
findings.description: 3 external script(s) lack SRI integrity attributes, making them vulnerable to CDN supply chain attacks.
findings.remediation: Add integrity and crossorigin attributes to all external scripts. Use https://www.srihash.org/ to generate hashes.
